--- title: "An Orientation to R" subtitle: "Session 2 | Getting comfortable with R and RStudio" author: "Dr Ashwini Kalantri" date: "2026-03-17" date-format: DD MMM YYYY institute: "Professor of Community Medicine, MGIMS, Sevagram" format: revealjs: theme: [default, ../../custom-theme.scss] slide-number: true footer: "R for HTA (Basics) â€” RRC-HTA, AIIMS Bhopal" logo: "" transition: fade width: 1280 height: 720 code-overflow: wrap highlight-style: github fig-align: center execute: echo: true eval: true warning: false message: false --- # âš ď¸Ź Warning âš ď¸Ź {auto-animate="true"} This is **not** an R programming course. # Goal {auto-animate="true"} When you see R code: - You are not `intimidated` by it - You `recognise` what it is doing - You can `run` it and see output - You can `change` an input value # The RStudio Interface ## {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted" data-menu-title="The Rstudio Interface"} ![](files/rstudio_1.png) ## {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted" data-menu-title="The Rstudio Interface"} ![](files/rstudio_2.png) # The Workflow ```{mermaid} %%| label: fig-workflow %%| fig-cap: The workshop workflow flowchart LR A["READ
the code
(understand logic)"]:::blue --> B["MODIFY
a parameter
(change a number)"]:::green --> C["RUN
the chunk
(click play)"]:::orange --> D["INTERPRET
the output
(what changed?)"]:::red D -. "Try again" .-> B classDef blue fill:#4e79a7,color:#fff classDef green fill:#59a14f,color:#fff classDef orange fill:#f28e2b,color:#fff classDef red fill:#e15759,color:#fff ``` ## Working with .qmd Files All workshop materials are in **Quarto (.qmd)** files: - **Text** â€” explanations - **Code chunks** â€” grey blocks with R code - **Outputs** â€” results, tables, plots . . . **To work with a .qmd file:** 1. Open it in RStudio 2. Read the text explanations 3. Run code chunks one at a time (click the green play button â–¶) 4. See the output appear below each chunk 5. Modify values and re-run # Your first `code` {auto-animate="true"} ::: {.cell output-location='fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} print("Coding for better health decisions.") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Coding for better health decisions." ``` ::: ::: ## Commenting {auto-animate="true"} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Print the text print("Coding for better health decisions.") print("Coding for better health decisions.") # Print the text # Multi-line comment # about printing the text print("Coding for better health decisions.") ``` ::: # Assignment {auto-animate="true"} Assign a `value` to a `name` with `<-` ::: {.cell output-location='fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} text <- "Coding for better health decisions." print(text) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Coding for better health decisions." ``` ::: ::: You may see `->` or `=` used for assignment. # Operators ## Arithmetic {auto-animate="true"} Addition ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 2 + 5 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 7 ``` ::: ::: Subtraction ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 73 - 32 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 41 ``` ::: ::: Multiplication ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 47 * 7 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 329 ``` ::: ::: Division ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 86 / 3 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 28.66667 ``` ::: ::: Exponentiation ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 8^2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 64 ``` ::: ::: ## Relational {auto-animate="true"} Greater ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 5 > 6 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] FALSE ``` ::: ::: Less than ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 5 < 6 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] TRUE ``` ::: ::: Equal ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 6 == 6 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] TRUE ``` ::: ::: ## Relational {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted"} Greater or equal ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 8 >= 5 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] TRUE ``` ::: ::: Less than or equal ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 7 <= 10 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] TRUE ``` ::: ::: Not Equal ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} 9 != 10 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] TRUE ``` ::: ::: # Objects {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted"} ## Vectors {auto-animate="true"} ::: {.cell output-location='fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} vec <- c(2, 4, 6, 8, 3, 5.5) vec ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 2.0 4.0 6.0 8.0 3.0 5.5 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell output-location='fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} costs <- c(treatment_A = 5000, treatment_B = 3000, treatment_C = 7000) costs ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` treatment_A treatment_B treatment_C 5000 3000 7000 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell output-location='fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} vec[5] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 3 ``` ::: ::: ## Matrix {auto-animate="true"} ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} mat <- matrix(1:36, nrow = 6, ncol = 6, byrow = F) mat ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6] [1,] 1 7 13 19 25 31 [2,] 2 8 14 20 26 32 [3,] 3 9 15 21 27 33 [4,] 4 10 16 22 28 34 [5,] 5 11 17 23 29 35 [6,] 6 12 18 24 30 36 ``` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} mat[3,5] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 27 ``` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} mat[,5] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 25 26 27 28 29 30 ``` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} mat[5,] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 5 11 17 23 29 35 ``` ::: ::: ## Multiplying Matrices {auto-animate="true"} ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} mat1 <- matrix(c(3, 5, 1, 9), nrow = 2, ncol = 2, byrow = F) mat1 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [,1] [,2] [1,] 3 1 [2,] 5 9 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} mat2 <- matrix(c(7, 4, 2, 8), nrow = 2, ncol = 2, byrow = F) mat2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [,1] [,2] [1,] 7 2 [2,] 4 8 ``` ::: ::: . . . Multiplying the matrices with `%*%` ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} mat1 %*% mat2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [,1] [,2] [1,] 25 14 [2,] 71 82 ``` ::: ::: ## Data Frames {auto-animate="true"} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} age <- c(12,24,NA,23,65,33) # create age vector gender <- c("M","F","F","M","M","F") #create gender vector occu <- factor(c(1,4,3,2,4,5), #occupation levels = c(1:5), labels = c("Unemp","Service","Student","Business","Prof")) #date of birth dob <- c(as.Date("1993-01-16"),as.Date("1963-12-24"),as.Date("1971-01-05"), as.Date("1982-11-11"),as.Date("1984-05-15"),as.Date("1999-03-07")) #create data frame df <- data.frame(age,gender,occu,dob) ``` ::: ## Data Frames {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted"} ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} df ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` age gender occu dob 1 12 M Unemp 1993-01-16 2 24 F Business 1963-12-24 3 NA F Student 1971-01-05 4 23 M Service 1982-11-11 5 65 M Business 1984-05-15 6 33 F Prof 1999-03-07 ``` ::: ::: . . . Accessing variables with `$` ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} df$age ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 12 24 NA 23 65 33 ``` ::: ::: # Functions {auto-animate="true"} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} function_name(argument1 = value1, argument2 = value2, ...) ``` ::: ## Functions {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted"} ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} mean(x = df$age, na.rm = TRUE) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 31.4 ``` ::: ::: # Packages {auto-animate="true"} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} install.packages("dplyr") ``` ::: ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} library(dplyr) dplyr::glimpse(df) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Rows: 6 Columns: 4 $ age 12, 24, NA, 23, 65, 33 $ gender "M", "F", "F", "M", "M", "F" $ occu Unemp, Business, Student, Service, Business, Prof $ dob 1993-01-16, 1963-12-24, 1971-01-05, 1982-11-11, 1984-05-15, 199â€¦ ``` ::: ::: . . . Install once, load every time # Pipes {auto-animate="true"} `|>` or `%>%` ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} df |> select(age, dob, occu) |> summarise( mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` mean_age 1 31.4 ``` ::: ::: # Importing Data {auto-animate="true"} ## Importing Data with GUI {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted"} ![](files/import.png) ## Importing Data with Code {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted"} CSV ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} data <- read.csv("files/data.csv") ``` ::: Excel ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(readxl) data <- read_excel("files/data.xlsx") ``` ::: Stata, SPSS ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(haven) data <- read_sav("files/data.sav") data <- read_dta("files/data.dta") ``` ::: ## A Swiss-Army Knife for Data I/O {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted"} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(rio) data <- rio::import("files/data.xlsx") data <- rio::import("files/data.csv") data <- rio::import("files/data.sav") data <- rio::import("files/data.dta") ``` ::: # Loops ## For loops ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} for (i in 1:nrow(data)) { bmi <- round(data$wt[i] / (((data$ht[i] / 100))^2), 2) cat("BMI of Record No", i, "is", bmi, "\n") } ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` BMI of Record No 1 is 21.63 BMI of Record No 2 is 20.62 BMI of Record No 3 is 23.7 BMI of Record No 4 is 15.62 BMI of Record No 5 is NA BMI of Record No 6 is 18.76 BMI of Record No 7 is 14.2 BMI of Record No 8 is 27.02 BMI of Record No 9 is 19.72 BMI of Record No 10 is 21.22 BMI of Record No 11 is 22.52 BMI of Record No 12 is 24.08 BMI of Record No 13 is 15.01 BMI of Record No 14 is 16.43 BMI of Record No 15 is 21.66 BMI of Record No 16 is 15.87 BMI of if(condition) { TRUE } else { FALSE } ``` ::: ## Conditional Logic {auto-animate="true" visibility="uncounted"} ::: {.cell output-location='column-fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} value <- 5000 if(value > 200) { cat("High") } else { cat("Low") } ``` ::: # Graphs ## ggplot ::: {.cell output-location='fragment'} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) ggplot(data, aes(x = ht, y = wt, colour = bmi)) + geom_point(size = 2) + labs(x = "Height", y = "Weight", title = "Height vs Weight") + theme_minimal() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](slides-session2_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-41-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Three Things to Remember 1. **Values are stored with `<-`** - Change them by editing the number . . . 2. **Code runs top to bottom** - Run chunks in order . . . 3. **If something breaks, read the error message** - It often tells you what went wrong Everything else you can look up â€” or ask an AI tool. ## Try It Yourself ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # YOUR TASK: Change these values and re-run cost_A <- 3000 cost_B <- 1500 qaly_A <- 0.82 qaly_B <- 0.75 # Calculate ICER icer <- (cost_A - cost_B) / (qaly_A - qaly_B) cat("ICER = â‚ą", round(icer, 0), "per QALY gained\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ICER = â‚ą 21429 per QALY gained ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} # Is it cost-effective? wtp <- 100000 if (icer < wtp) { cat("Cost-effective at WTP = â‚ą", format(wtp, big.mark=",", scientific = F), "\n") } else { cat("NOT cost-effective\n") } ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Cost-effective at WTP = â‚ą 100,000 ``` ::: :::

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
